text: How geography powers Iran’s grip on the Strait of Hormuz, despite U.S. blockade - The Washington Post
fullText: Even with a U.S. blockage, geography gives Iran an edge in the Strait of Hormuz, shaping control of a vital global chokepoint.

text: Israel and Lebanon set to meet for first direct talks in more than 30 years - NPR

text: Some people don’t lose weight with GLP-1s. Evidence is building that the drugs are helping anyway - CNN

text: Court orders DC judge to end criminal contempt inquiry into Trump officials involved in deportation flights - CNN
fullText: A divided federal appeals court on Tuesday ordered US District Judge James Boasberg to end his efforts to hold Trump administration officials accountable for flouting his orders in a high-stakes immigration case.

text: Lightweight cabriolet with a high-revving naturally aspirated engine and manual transmission - Porsche Newsroom
fullText: The Porsche GT family welcomes a particularly exciting new member. The 911 GT3 S/C combines a range of ‘driver's car’ qualities that have already delighted customers of the limited-edition 911 Speedster and 911 S/T models.

text: Ultraprocessed foods may hurt muscle health, study finds - NBC News
fullText: Eating too much ultraprocessed food could take a toll on muscle health, according to new research published Tuesday in the journal Radiology.

text: Fuel protests have Ireland's government facing possible no-confidence vote - NPR
fullText: The prime minister announced new tax cuts to try to end the crisis that began after the U.S.-Israel war on Iran led to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The government could face a no-confidence vote over its response to the fuel protests.

text: Lena Dunham: Adam Driver 'Verbally Aggressive' on 'Girls,' Threw Chair - Variety

fullText: Lena Dunham is opening up about engaging in infidelity toward the end of her relationship with Jack Antonoff in her explosive new memoir 'Famesick.'

text: Brian Hooker, husband of woman missing in Bahamas: "I won't be able to stop looking" - CBS News

text: Trump’s leaky blockade: ship sneaks through and talks with Iran resume - Fortune

text: What was that bright streak in the sky this morning? It wasn’t a comet or meteor - WSB-TV
fullText: Space X launched its 1,000th Starlink into low Earth orbit around 5:30 a.m. from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station. But people up and down the East Coast could see what’s called a “jellyfish” effect from the launch.
